author: Dr. Phong Le, MD
department: Primary Care — Charm City Asian Health Center
content:
ANNUAL PRIMARY CARE VISIT — 3/12/2015, 9:30 AM
Patient: Nguyen, Lan Thi (Age 60)
Insurance: Employer-sponsored PPO (still working at Baltimore Textile Co.)
Vietnamese-language visit; no third-party interpreter.

INTERVAL HISTORY (since 2014 annual):
Ms. Nguyen reports she has been generally well. Her major events of the past year:
- Husband Hai had a CHF hospitalization (3 weeks at Sinai) in November 2014 for fluid overload; he is doing better on optimized medical therapy.
- Continues full-time work as floor manager at the textile factory; no plans to retire until 65 (age-65 Medicare eligibility).
- Quit smoking in 2004 after her husband's MI; reports she has been completely smoke-free since (verified, ~14 pack-years lifetime). Says her husband restarted briefly in 2008 then stopped again.
- Alcohol: continues 1–2 drinks/day, no change. AUDIT-C today = 3.
- Tai chi 2x/week; walking to work.

No new complaints today. Mild right knee discomfort with prolonged standing — has been ongoing for ~5 years now, worse some days than others. Manages with acetaminophen and occasional ibuprofen.

Review of systems otherwise negative: no chest pain, no dyspnea, no palpitations, no syncope; no headaches; no dysphagia, no GI symptoms; no dysuria, no hematuria; no rash; no peripheral edema; no neurologic complaints.

PMH:
- Hypertension (diagnosed 4/2010, BP 142/86 initial; on amlodipine then switched to lisinopril 2012)
- Latent TB infection (positive PPD + IGRA 2010; never completed INH treatment — declined repeatedly given alcohol restriction and monthly monitoring burden, ultimately deferred)
- Right knee osteoarthritis (mild, conservative management)
- Post-menopausal

MEDS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (continued)
- Multivitamin daily
- Acetaminophen 500–1000 mg PO PRN
- Ibuprofen 400 mg PO PRN (~1–2x/week)

FAMILY UPDATE: Father deceased 1995 (MI 68). Mother deceased 2008 (CVA 81). Brother (older, age 60) heavy smoker, otherwise healthy. Brother (younger, age 55) recently diagnosed T2DM. Sister (age 53) healthy.

VITALS: BP 132/80, HR 76, RR 14, T 98.2, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 60.4 kg (133 lb, up 2 kg from last visit). BMI 25.5.

EXAM:
General: Well-appearing, no distress.
HEENT: Normal. Thyroid not enlarged.
CV: RRR, normal S1/S2, no murmur. No JVD. Peripheral pulses 2+.
Lungs: CTAB.
Abdomen: Soft, NT/ND.
MSK: Right knee with mild medial-joint-line tenderness, no effusion, full ROM with crepitus. No deformity. Otherwise unremarkable.
Neuro / Skin / Psych: Normal.

LABS (drawn today, results back same week):
- BMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 16, Cr 1.0 (eGFR 58 in this age group — note slight increase from 0.9 in 2010), Glu 96
- LFTs: AST 26, ALT 22, ALP 68, T-bili 0.5, Albumin 4.2 — all normal
- LIPID PANEL: Total chol 224, HDL 48, LDL 168 (HIGH), TG 142
- HbA1c 5.4 (normal)
- TSH 2.41 (normal)
- CBC: WBC 5.8, Hgb 12.6, Plt 248 (normal)
- UA: bland, no protein

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. HYPERTENSION — well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg. BP today 132/80. Continue.
2. NEW: HYPERLIPIDEMIA — LDL 168, elevated. Discussed cardiovascular risk; she is 60yo with HTN + family history of CHD (father MI). 10-year ASCVD risk estimate ~10–12%. Recommended starting moderate-intensity statin. Patient agreed.
   → Start atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily. Will recheck lipids and LFTs at 3-month follow-up.
3. RIGHT KNEE OA — stable; conservative management continued. Discussed eventual TKA likely needed; she would prefer to delay.
4. LATENT TB INFECTION — still untreated. Re-discussed today through the Vietnamese interpreter line (Dr. Le summary). Patient reaffirmed she does not want INH due to alcohol and monitoring burden. Discussed reactivation risk (low but real, lifetime). Documented declination. Will reassess if symptoms develop or risk factors change.
5. HEALTH MAINTENANCE — mammogram completed 2/2015, negative. Colonoscopy 2014 with 2 small adenomatous polyps removed, scheduled rescreen 2019. Flu shot today (declined; will offer again in fall). Pneumovax not yet due.
6. SMOKING — confirmed continued cessation, ~11 years smoke-free. Encouraged.
7. ALCOHOL — counseled briefly. Patient does not see daily 1–2 drinks as problematic; she declines to change at this time. Continue annual screening.

Follow-up: 3 months (lipid + LFT check on statin). Annual physical again in 12 months.

Visit time: 50 minutes.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Phong Le, MD
Charm City Asian Health Center
Date: 3/12/2015
